notes: Reproduced on all tested versions (3.119.0, 3.119.2, main source). The SVG canvas writes the closing </svg> tag during canvas disposal/destruction — this is Skia's native behavior for finalizing the SVG document. The reporter reads MemoryStream.ToArray() before the canvas is disposed because 'using var' defers disposal to end of scope. When reading AFTER disposal, the closing tag is present. The triage analysis is correct: this is a disposal ordering issue in the reporter's code, not a SkiaSharp bug.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console repro project with SkiaSharp 3.119.0
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3520 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3520 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.119.0
reproductionSteps.output:
The template "Console App" was created successfully.
PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp' version '3.119.0' added.

filesCreated.description: Console project targeting net8.0 with SkiaSharp 3.119.0 package reference
Reproduction code testing SVG output before and after canvas disposal. Test 1 reads MemoryStream before canvas disposal (reporter's pattern). Test 2 reads after disposal (correct pattern).

reproductionSteps.description: Run repro - verify SVG output before and after canvas disposal. Test 1 (reporter's pattern) shows missing closing tag. Test 2 (correct pattern) shows tag present.
reproductionSteps.output:
--- Test 1: Read stream BEFORE canvas disposal ---
Has closing </svg> tag: False

--- Test 2: Read stream AFTER canvas disposal ---
Has closing </svg> tag: True

versionResults.notes: SVG missing closing </svg> tag when MemoryStream read before canvas disposal. Tag present when read after disposal.
Same behavior as 3.119.0 — closing tag missing before disposal, present after disposal.
Same behavior on main branch source build — closing tag written on canvas destruction, not before.

actionability.reason: The SVG canvas writes the closing </svg> tag on disposal — this is Skia's native behavior. The reporter's code reads the stream before canvas disposal due to 'using var' scoping. The existing test SvgCanvasSavesFile confirms the correct pattern. This is a disposal ordering issue, not a bug.

actions.description: Explain disposal ordering and provide workaround with correct code pattern
Close as by-design behavior — SVG finalization happens on canvas disposal

workarounds: Use a scoped 'using' block for the canvas instead of 'using var', then call ToArray() after the block ends
Pass MemoryStream directly to SKSvgCanvas.Create instead of wrapping in SKManagedWStream manually
Explicitly call canvas.Dispose() before reading the stream data
